title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Chihuahuan Desert peyote origin
description: Coahuila Mexico — Huichol (Wixáritari) pilgrimage route to Wirikuta sacred site. Lophophora williamsii grows only in this specific desert ecosystem.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Chihuahuan Desert peyote origin
description: San Luis Potosí — Wirikuta UNESCO-recognized Huichol sacred territory.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Chihuahuan Desert peyote origin
description: South Texas — Native American Church peyote gardens; legal harvest continues under federal exemption.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Amazonian ayahuasca
description: Manaus Brazil — Indigenous ayahuasca traditions (Shipibo, Yawanawa, Yaminawa + dozens more peoples).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Amazonian ayahuasca
description: Iquitos Peru — modern ayahuasca tourism hub; ~$200M/year industry raises ethical questions about extractive 'plant medicine' markets.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Mesoamerican psilocybin mushrooms
description: Oaxaca — Mazatec María Sabina's velada ceremony. 1955 Life magazine article + R. Gordon Wasson's visit triggers Western psychedelic awareness.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: 20th-century synthetic psychedelics
description: Basel Switzerland — 1938 Albert Hofmann (Sandoz) synthesizes LSD. 1943 Bicycle Day.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: 20th-century synthetic psychedelics
description: Amsterdam — MDMA + psychedelics tolerated in coffeeshop / smart-shop model.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: 20th-century synthetic psychedelics
description: Washington DC — 1970 Controlled Substances Act Schedule I. 1985 MDMA emergency ban. 2024 FDA rejects MDMA-PTSD approval.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Psychedelic Renaissance research
description: Harvard / Timothy Leary 1960s → Johns Hopkins (Roland Griffiths) 2006+ → MAPS clinical trials → 2023–24 breakthrough therapies.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Psychedelic Renaissance research
description: Johns Hopkins — Center for Psychedelic and Consciousness Research opens 2019.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Psychedelic Renaissance research
description: Portland Oregon — 2020 Measure 109 first legal US psilocybin services. 2023 clinics opened.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Indigenous + scientific names
LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Indigenous + scientific names
description: Quechua — 'ayahuasca' = 'vine of the soul'. Preserved unchanged in all languages.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Indigenous + scientific names
description: Mazatec — 'ki-tʃo-ki' (children, referring to mushrooms). Replaced in English by Greek 'psilocybe'.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Oregon + Colorado — legal psilocybin programs (medical + service settings).

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: California — decriminalized plant medicines in several cities.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Netherlands — smart-shop magic truffles, legal grey zone.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Peru — ayahuasca retreat tourism, $200M+ annual industry.
